key_insight: PNOT reconstructs divertor temperature fields by combining physics-aware graph structure, neural operator attention, and derivative-consistent learning to improve accuracy and physical plausibility.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, real-time temperature-field estimation for tungsten monoblock divertors in EAST, supporting thermal safety monitoring and control before melting or damage occurs.
method: Models boundary heat-flux relations as a structured graph with graph attention to capture spatial physical dependencies.
Uses a physics-aware neural operator transformer to aggregate query points with similar physical conditions and represent heat diffusion.
Adds a gradient-constrained Sobolev regularization loss to keep predicted temperature values consistent with their derivatives.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that experimental results show the added physics constraints improve prediction accuracy while preserving physical consistency.
watch_item: Performance likely depends on the quality of boundary-condition graph construction and may require careful validation before deployment on unseen operating regimes.
paper_title: Temperature Field Reconstruction of Tungsten Monoblock Divertor on EAST using Physics-aware Neural Operator Transformer
